verdict_reasoning: TMED9 is a p24 family cargo receptor that cycles between ER and Golgi, mediating COPI/COPII-dependent transport. It is an endomembrane-resident type I TM protein with no documented stable plasma membrane pool. Walking the contextual buckets: no documented cancer/cell-death/activation-induced outer-leaflet display; no germline/developmental narrow surface restriction; not a lysosomal TM protein (ER-Golgi cycling, not lysosomal exocytosis); no documented PM minority pool from surface biotinylation or flow on intact cells; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to a surface scaffold. No surface-directed clinical programs. Compartment is canonically ER-Golgi.
